untranslatable_reason: Signal requires dynamic detection of support/resistance breakouts and role reversal. The allowed indicators (Pivot points, Moving Averages, Donchian) can approximate support/resistance levels, but cannot programmatically identify: (1) which level is broken, (2) confirmation of breakout, (3) subsequent return to broken level, and (4) rejection at that level. These require pattern recognition or custom support/resistance detection logic not available in the indicator set. Pivot points (r1, s1) used as proxy but do not capture the signal's dynamic breakout-and-rejection mechanism.
translation_notes: Attempted translation using pivot points (r1 for broken resistance, s1 for broken support) with elevated volume as rejection confirmation. However, the core logic—detecting when a support/resistance level is broken, confirming the move, then detecting a return and rejection—requires multi-bar pattern matching and level identification that exceeds the capability of point-in-time indicator comparisons. Consider implementing a custom support/resistance detector or using machine learning-based level detection.
